import sys
from bisect import bisect_left

def solve():
    input_data = sys.stdin.read().split()
    idx = 0
    t = int(input_data[idx]); idx += 1
    for _ in range(t):
        m = int(input_data[idx]); idx += 1
        n = int(input_data[idx]); idx += 1
        p = []
        for i in range(m):
            row = [int(input_data[idx+j]) for j in range(n)]
            idx += n
            p.append(row)
        
        # For each friend j, max joy = max over shops
        # Binary search on alpha
        # Collect all unique values
        vals = sorted(set(v for row in p for v in row))
        
        def feasible(alpha):
            # Every friend coverable
            for j in range(n):
                if max(p[i][j] for i in range(m)) < alpha:
                    return False
            # Some shop covers >= 2 friends
            for i in range(m):
                cnt = sum(1 for j in range(n) if p[i][j] >= alpha)
                if cnt >= 2:
                    return True
            return False
        
        lo, hi = 0, len(vals) - 1
        ans = vals[0] if feasible(vals[0]) else 0
        while lo <= hi:
            mid = (lo + hi) // 2
            if feasible(vals[mid]):
                ans = vals[mid]
                lo = mid + 1
            else:
                hi = mid - 1
        print(ans)

solve()

import sys
input = sys.stdin.readline

def solve():
    # skip empty lines
    line = input().split()
    while not line:
        line = input().split()
    m, n = int(line[0]), int(line[1])
    p = []
    for i in range(m):
        row = list(map(int, input().split()))
        p.append(row)
    
    # Binary search on alpha
    # Collect all values for binary search bounds
    all_vals = sorted(set(v for row in p for v in row))
    
    def feasible(alpha):
        # For each friend, check if any shop has p[i][j] >= alpha
        friend_covered = [False] * n
        shop_covers_two = False
        for i in range(m):
            cnt = 0
            for j in range(n):
                if p[i][j] >= alpha:
                    friend_covered[j] = True
                    cnt += 1
            if cnt >= 2:
                shop_covers_two = True
        return all(friend_covered) and shop_covers_two
    
    lo, hi = 0, len(all_vals) - 1
    ans = 0
    while lo <= hi:
        mid = (lo + hi) // 2
        if feasible(all_vals[mid]):
            ans = all_vals[mid]
            lo = mid + 1
        else:
            hi = mid - 1
    print(ans)

t = int(input())
for _ in range(t):
    solve()

def solve():

    for _ in range(t):
        m = int(input_data[idx]); idx += 1
        n = int(input_data[idx]); idx += 1
        p = []
        for i in range(m):
            row = [int(input_data[idx+j]) for j in range(n)]
            idx += n
            p.append(row)
        
        # For each friend j, max joy = max over shops
        # Binary search on alpha
        # Collect all unique values
        vals = sorted(set(v for row in p for v in row))
        
        def feasible(alpha):
            # Every friend coverable
            for j in range(n):
                if max(p[i][j] for i in range(m)) < alpha:
                    return False
            # Some shop covers >= 2 friends
            for i in range(m):
                cnt = sum(1 for j in range(n) if p[i][j] >= alpha)
                if cnt >= 2:
                    return True
            return False
        
        lo, hi = 0, len(vals) - 1
        ans = vals[0] if feasible(vals[0]) else 0
        while lo <= hi:
            mid = (lo + hi) // 2
            if feasible(vals[mid]):
                ans = vals[mid]
                lo = mid + 1
            else:
                hi = mid - 1
        print(ans)

import sys
input = sys.stdin.readline

def solve():
    # skip empty lines
    line = input().split()
    while not line:
        line = input().split()
    m, n = int(line[0]), int(line[1])
    p = []
    for i in range(m):
        row = list(map(int, input().split()))
        p.append(row)
    
    # Binary search on alpha
    # Collect all values for binary search bounds
    all_vals = sorted(set(v for row in p for v in row))
    
    def feasible(alpha):
        # For each friend, check if any shop has p[i][j] >= alpha
        friend_covered = [False] * n
        shop_covers_two = False
        for i in range(m):
            cnt = 0
            for j in range(n):
                if p[i][j] >= alpha:
                    friend_covered[j] = True
                    cnt += 1
            if cnt >= 2:
                shop_covers_two = True
        return all(friend_covered) and shop_covers_two
    
    lo, hi = 0, len(all_vals) - 1
    ans = 0
    while lo <= hi:
        mid = (lo + hi) // 2
        if feasible(all_vals[mid]):
            ans = all_vals[mid]
            lo = mid + 1
        else:
            hi = mid - 1
    print(ans)

t = int(input())
for _ in range(t):
    solve()
